meaning: adj.消费的,消耗性的,浪费的n.肺病患者
examples: We are living in a consumptive and optional times.
This change has brought with it consumptive habit changes.
People's consumptive habit is changing rapidly because of the Internet.
Relative to actual annual consumptive usage, there is already a greater inventory of gold than there is of any other commodity.
"Data centers are hugely consumptive forces, " said Kevin Klustner, chief executive of Verdiem, a Seattle-based start-up that has developed energy-efficiency software for computer systems.
